terms.s1Body: These terms of service ("Terms") govern access to and use of the Avilyre service (the "Service"), a SaaS platform for supplier risk management and anticipation, available at avilyre.com. The Service is operated by Abdoulaye Chaibou, an individual operator based in Morocco ("we", "the Operator").
terms.s2Body: By creating an account or using the Service, you accept these Terms in full. If you do not agree, you must not use the Service. The Service is intended for professional (B2B) use; you warrant that you are acting within the scope of your professional activity.
terms.s3Body: Avilyre lets you reference suppliers, compute risk scores (financial, operational, geopolitical, ESG), configure alerts, import data, and access AI-powered features (analysis, prediction, OSINT enrichment). The Service is provided "as is" and may evolve or be improved without notice.
terms.s4Body: You are responsible for keeping your credentials confidential and for all activity carried out from your account. You agree to provide accurate information when creating your account and organization.
terms.s5Body1: A 14-day free trial is offered with no credit card required. After the trial, access is limited to a restricted free tier unless you subscribe to a paid plan (Starter, Pro, or Enterprise on request).
terms.s5Body2Middle: , our reseller and Merchant of Record. Paddle invoices in its own name, collects applicable tax, and issues the corresponding invoices; Paddle's payment terms (
terms.s5Body2After: ) apply in addition to these Terms for everything relating to the payment transaction itself.
terms.s5Body3Before: Subscriptions renew automatically at each billing period (monthly or annual) until cancelled. See our
terms.s6Body: You may cancel your subscription at any time from your billing area; cancellation takes effect at the end of the period already paid for. We reserve the right to suspend or terminate an account in case of breach of these Terms or fraudulent use.
terms.s7Body: The Service, its code, brand and content are the property of the Operator. You retain full ownership of the data you import or create within the Service ("your data").
terms.s8Before: The processing of personal data is described in our
terms.s9Body: We use reasonable means to ensure the availability and reliability of the Service, without warranting it will be free of interruptions or errors. The risk analyses, scores and predictions generated (including by artificial intelligence) are provided for informational and decision-support purposes only; they do not replace your own professional analysis and judgment. To the extent permitted by applicable law, our liability is limited to the amounts paid for the subscription over the past twelve months.
terms.s10Body: We may amend these Terms; any material change will be notified to you by email or through the Service. Continued use of the Service after notification constitutes acceptance.
terms.s11Body: These Terms are governed by Moroccan law, without prejudice to mandatory consumer protection or personal data provisions applicable in the user's country.
terms.s12Before: For any question regarding these Terms:

privacy.s1Before: The controller for personal data collected through Avilyre is Abdoulaye Chaibou, an individual operator based in Morocco. Contact:
s2Items: Account data: name, email, password (hashed), company, role.
Business data you enter or import: supplier records, risk assessments, documents, contacts.
Billing data, processed directly by Paddle.com (we do not store your card details).
Technical data: IP address, connection logs, strictly necessary cookies (see our cookie policy).
s3Items: Providing the Service and performing the contract (Terms) — legal basis: contractual performance.
Billing and accounting obligations — legal basis: legal obligation and contractual performance.
Transactional emails and alerts you configure — legal basis: contractual performance.
Improving the Service and security — legal basis: legitimate interest.
privacy.s4Intro: Your data may be processed by the following providers, strictly within the scope of their service:
s4Items: Supabase (database hosting, authentication, files) — European Union region.
Paddle.com (Merchant of Record — payment, billing, tax).
Groq and OpenRouter (language model processing for analysis and AI copilot features — content sent to these providers is limited to what's needed for the requested feature).
privacy.s4Outro: Some of these providers (Groq, OpenRouter, Paddle, Vercel) may process data outside the European Union; in that case, the transfer relies on the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses or an equivalent mechanism provided by these processors.
privacy.s5Body: Your data is kept for the duration of your contractual relationship with us, then archived or deleted within a reasonable period after your account is closed, unless a longer legal retention obligation applies (accounting, billing).
privacy.s6Before: In accordance with the GDPR and, where applicable, Moroccan law No. 09-08, you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict, object to, and port your personal data. You can exercise these rights by writing to
privacy.s6After: . If you reside in the European Union, you also have the right to lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority (in France, the CNIL).
privacy.s7Body: Your data is protected by encryption in transit (HTTPS) and at rest, as well as strict isolation between customer accounts (database-level RLS policies). No system being infallible, we cannot guarantee absolute security.
privacy.s8Body: This policy may be updated; the last-updated date appears at the top of this page. Any material change will be notified to you.

cookies.s1Body: A cookie is a small file stored on your device when you visit a website, used to retain information between visits or pages.
cookies.s2Intro: We exclusively use cookies that are strictly necessary for the Service to function — no advertising or third-party tracking cookie is set as of today:
s2Items: Session/authentication cookies (Supabase Auth) — keep you signed in to your account.
CSRF cookie — protects forms against request-forgery attacks.
Display preference (layout-style) — remembers your layout choice (sidebar or header).
Demo mode (vs-demo-mode) — active only when you visit the public read-only demo.
cookies.s3Body: Our payment provider Paddle.com may set its own technical cookies when you go through their hosted payment page, in accordance with their own policy. If we deploy audience-measurement or advertising-tracking tools in the future (for example for advertising campaigns), this page will be updated accordingly and a consent banner will be shown before any non-essential cookie is set.
cookies.s4Body: Since the cookies described above are strictly necessary for the Service to function, they are not subject to your prior consent. You can still delete them through your browser settings, at the risk of disrupting your connection to the Service.

refund.s1Body: Avilyre offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required. No payment is taken during this period; once it ends, access is limited to a restricted free tier unless you subscribe.
refund.s2Before: If you subscribe to a paid plan (Starter or Pro) and are not satisfied, you can request a full refund within
refund.s2After: of your first payment, no questions asked. After this period, payments already made are not refunded on a pro-rata basis, except where mandatory law applicable in your country provides otherwise.
refund.s3Before: You can cancel your subscription at any time from your billing area (
refund.s3After: ) or via the Paddle customer portal. Cancellation prevents the next renewal but does not refund the current period already paid for (except within the 14-day window described above). You keep access until the end of the period already paid for.
refund.s4Body: In case of a duplicate charge, an incorrect amount, or any other billing issue, contact us immediately — these situations are corrected in full, with no specific deadline.
refund.s5After: , our Merchant of Record, refunds are issued by Paddle to the same payment method used for the purchase, once the request is approved. How long it takes to appear on your bank statement depends on your bank (typically 5 to 10 business days).
refund.s6After: with your account email and the reason for your request. We reply within 48 business hours.
